question: How long do claims take to be paid?
text:
It depends on the type of claim. 
For exits (resignations) claims are paid within 14 days after the last contribution for the 
member has been receipted by the Fund and the Fund has received complete and final 
documentation. Remember that your last month's contribution to the Fund will only be paid 
by your employer by the 20th of the following month. So the Fund can only start processing 
your claim towards the end of the month after you have resigned, and then only if all your 
documents have been received, your tax clearance has been received from SARS and your 
bank account details have been verified. 
Example - If you resign at the end of February the Fund will only receive your February 
contribution by the 20th of March. After your contribution has been receipted and invested 
the Fund can only start to process your claim. 
Death claims are paid within 12 months.​
Funeral claims are paid within 48 hours.​
Disability claims are paid within 3 months.

question: What are the things that can go wrong that will prevent my claim from being paid?
text:
●​ If your employer does not inform the Fund that you have left (often claim forms are 
only received by the Fund a few months after the member's last working day). 
●​ If the member and employer does not sign the form, or the employer has not 
stamped the form. 
●​ If the employer does not complete and send the correct forms. 
●​ If the Fund does not have a valid tax number for the member. 
●​ If the employer makes an additional payment of a contribution after you have left. 
●​ If you get another job and are back working in the industry the month after you left 
your previous employer your money remains in the Fund and will not be paid out to 
you as you continue to be a member of the Fund. 
●​ If your employer is non-compliant in paying over your contributions to the Fund.

question: Where can I make a complaint?
text:
You should complain to the Fund directly by sending an email to the Principal Office of the 
Fund at compliance@salteb.co.za. 
The Principal Officer may address the complaint immediately or may need to institute an 
investigation to ascertain the causes that led to the complaint. Where necessary, the 
Principal Officer will refer the complaint to the Board of Trustees for consideration. 
If the Fund’s response deems to be unsatisfactory, or the Fund fails to address the 
complaint, the member has the right to refer the case to the Pension Funds Adjudicator. 
There is no charge to lodge a complaint with the Adjudicator’s Office. You can contact the 
Adjudicator's Office on: E-mail address: enquiries@pfa.org.za 
Tel: 012 346 1738/012 748 4000 Fax: 086 693 7472

question: How much money do I have in the Fund?
text:
You will receive an annual Benefit Statement in November each year that will show your 
value in the Fund as at end of February of that year. End of February is the financial 
year-end of the Fund. 
If you cannot wait for your benefit statement you can either: 
●​ Call the Fund Call Centre on 087 405 6377 
●​ Register on a new online tool that helps members see their Fund values, help with 
retirement planning and much more! https://transport.benefitcounsellor.com/login 
To register type: transport.benefitcounsellor.com in your internet browser and follow the 7 
easy steps. Once registered, you can dial *120*15552# or WhatsApp 087 240 7004 to see 
your fund balance.

question: Will I receive an SMS to say you have received my documents?
text:
The Fund receives between 4000 and 7000 claim forms per month. These are sent directly 
to TSRF Front Offices by the employers. TSRF makes sure that the documents are 
complete. TSRF will send an SMS to the members to confirm that documents have been 
received and will ask for outstanding information if that is required.

question: What supporting documents do I need for a claim?
text:
Call 087 405 6377 to find out. The claim form also contains a list of supporting documents 
that must be attached when sending in the claim.

question: Please explain the claims process?
text:
STEP 1: Fill in the claim form Where to get a claim form: 
●​ www.tsrf.co.za or click here. 
●​ Call 087 405 6377 or visit the TSRF walk-in-centres in Braamfontein, Durban and 
Bellville (Cape Town) 
●​ Employer ‘s HR department 
●​ All claim documents to be sent to members@tsrf.salteb.co.za 
The claim form includes a list of supporting documents that must be attached when sending 
in the claim. 
STEP 2: Get the employer to sign the claim form. Your employer or HR department will need 
to complete and sign the claim form so that the claim can be processed and payment made. 
Disability claims require a medical certificate (confidential Medical Report completed by a 
physician) to be verified by the Funds appointed medical provider. Death and Funeral Claims 
require certified copies of the Death certificate for verification and supporting documents as 
required on the claim form. 
STEP 3: Submit Claim Submit the signed and stamped claim form with the required 
supporting documents to the TSRF walk-in centres in Braamfontein, Durban or Bellville. Or 
submit via fax or e-mail: Email: members@tsrf.salteb.co.za Fax: 086 593 0006 
Kindly address all Transport Sector Retirement Fund claim related enquiries to: 
members@tsrf.salteb.co.za 
Claim queries can be made via the Call Centre: Tel: 087 405 6377 
The claim process: 
1.​ All documents are received and verified by Sanlam 
2.​ TSRF forwards completed documents to SALT EB 
3.​ SALT EB requests bank verification
4.​ Tax directive is applied for via SARS by SALT EB 
5.​ Payment is made by SALT EB

question: How long do death claims take to process?
text:
The Pension Funds Act allows 12 months for death benefits to be paid. The Board of 
Trustees must make sure who all the dependants are and t what extent they were dependent 
on the member. This may take a few months to finalise. 
Make sure that you complete a Beneficiary Nomination form to identify your dependents. You 
will find this form on this website under Forms Download.

question: How can I check my balance?
text:
●​ Call the Fund Call Centre on 087 405 6377 
●​ Register on a new online tool that helps members see their Fund values, help with 
retirement planning and much more! https://transport.benefitcounsellor.com/login 
To register type: transport.benefitcounsellor.com in your internet browser and follow the 7 
easy steps. Once registered, you can dial *120*15552# or WhatsApp 087 240 7004 to see 
your fund balance.

question: When will sick and leave pay be paid?
text:
Sick and leave pay is paid by the Bargaining Council and the Fund can therefore not assist 
members with this enquiry. Please visit your nearest NBCRFLI Office.

question: How will I know if my employer is paying over my deductions?
text:
You will be informed via SMS when your employer has not paid over contributions to the 
Fund for 60 days. You can also contact the Fund on 087 405 6377 to speak to an agent.

question: How do I get a benefit statement?
text:
Benefit statements are distributed to your employer in November each year. Please ask your 
employer for a copy of your statement. Benefit statements are also available on the TSRF 
Online Benefit Counsellor.

question: How often do I get a benefit statement?
text: Once a year in November.

question: My employer will not sign my exit form what must I do?
text:
Please inform the Fund by either calling the Call Centre on 087 405 6377 or send an email 
to: members@tsrf.salteb.co.za.

question: I was fired/retrenched and my employers are missing. How can I claim?
text:
Please inform the Fund by either calling the Call Centre on 087 405 6377 or send an email 
to: members@tsrf.salteb.co.za or visiting one of the Fund's walk-in centres.

question: My employer is not paying my contributions. What can I do?
text: The Fund will take legal action against the employer - no action is required from members.

question: What is your website address?
text: www.tsrf.co.za

question: Where can I find a walk-in centre?
text:
20 De Korte St Braamfontein Gauteng or 116 Edward St Oakdale Bellville Western Cape & 
300 Anton Lembede St 6th Floor Office 606 Delta Towers Durban.

question: Does the Fund only make 1 payment on a claim?
text:
In the case of a death claim - each dependent/nominee that receives money will receive a 
payment into their own account. So yes, more than 1 payment is made. 
In case of a retirement claim or exit claim usually only 1 payment is made. The exception is 
where the employer is behind in the payment of contributions. Then the Fund will make a 
follow-up payment to the member once the employer pays the outstanding contributions. 
This means that the Fund will pay your benefit when you claim and will then make another 
payment once your employer has paid over the outstanding contributions.

question: My money is short. How much tax do I pay?
text:
At retirement only the first R 500,000 is tax free. 
When you resign and exit the Fund before retirement only the first R 25,000 is tax free. 
These tax-free amounts are cumulative over your whole lifetime. If you have resigned before 
and received the R 25,000 tax free it means that with a subsequent exit all your money will 
be taxed.

question: I need to get my ITC3 certificate. How can I access this?
text:
A payment letter and IT3 certificate is posted to the address you gave on your exit form. If 
you did not receive yours, you can Call 087 405 6377.

question: How does the fund manage our money?
text:
Contributions (that are allocated towards retirement savings) are invested by the Fund over 
the term of your membership. The interest earned on the contributions invested is also 
added. 
The Fund invests your contributions in Life Stage portfolios based on your age and 
remaining term to retirement as follows: 
Member 54 and younger - Growth Portfolio​
Members 55 to 63 - Conservative Portfolio​
Members 64 and older - Cash Portfolio

question: Where can I access the Rules of the Fund?
text:
Go to About the Fund, Rules to read the Rules of the Fund. 
Any question posed that the BOT does not have an answer for must have the reply:
Sorry we are unable to assist with your enquiry. Please contact the Fund Call Centre on 087 
405 6377 or visit one of the Fund Walk-in Centres. Go to our Contact page to see the details 
of the various Walk-in Centres.

TSRF HUB Report Period: February 2026 Claims submitted Total Claims submitted for both TSRF and RFLIPF for the month are 432. We are unable to confirm the number of claims submitted directly at fund offices from the skeptical members, however upon following up with some of our members we find that they have submitted them at the various fund offices. Submission listing has been sent to SALT for verification and payment purposes. Phone Records Call records as per Niport is 7482 excluding calls made from our mobile numbers. CHALLENGES EPIC We are still experiencing some challenges with Epic (see below) • No Option to Exit for some claims • Few of the RFLIPF members are not on the system • We are unable to submit Death withdrawals Challenges The challenges below have previously been raised, yet to date not much has changed or been implemented. We have asked that our claims be flagged when the members go to submit physically as they are doubtful. Only upon following up, are we given a response. Flagging these submissions is important for ZDM monthly invoices and for accurate reporting. Traced members who contact the fund to query the status of their claims are being informed that ZDM has not submitted their documents. Upon requesting feedback, we are informed that those members have previously been paid and have no benefits due to them yet the data allocated shows a fund credit. We have members who indicated that the time frame for the process and payments is not what they have been informed about which is 4-6 weeks- with no notification of delays or reasons to manage member expectations.

Members are being turned away at the Bellville Fund office and informed that they must go back to the company they worked at to claim there. Some fund offices are still not aware that ZDM has been appointed as the tracer for the fund. We have found that the claim form which was issued to ZDM to provide to the members is being changed at some of the fund offices. We request that the above-mentioned issues be resolved as a matter of urgency as the administrators spend a lot of time tackling queries instead of tracing members. Fund Credit The below challenge has been raised in all our reports. A crucial problem when we are meant to provide members with confirmation letters is fund credit of less than R2 000 which is required to obtain estate late account. Clarity about this matter has been requested from the fund, however we have not received a directive, and we have been dealing with such claims on a case-by-case basis. Majority of these come from the RFLIPF data. Members are being told they cannot claim as they have no funds yet, according to data allocated there is fund credit available to the members. Previously Paid Members ZDM received data which was sanitized. The data has two tabs, separating paid claims and still to be traced. The team has found that some members have been paid yet are reflected under traceable tab. A concern we have is that we were informed that ZDM would be paid simultaneously when the fund pays the member, yet no one has advised on ZDM payment in instances when the member was previously traced and paid or in instances when the fund credit is low. We have numerous of these claims. Recurring Challenge The team has indicated that members

be paid simultaneously when the fund pays the member, yet no one has advised on ZDM payment in instances when the member was previously traced and paid or in instances when the fund credit is low. We have numerous of these claims. Recurring Challenge The team has indicated that members have blocked and flagged the land line number (as a spam). We are currently utilizing three mobile numbers as another resort to ascertain that they are tracing the correct member. Incorrect Data A challenge which we are still finding is that off incorrect/invalid ID numbers or incorrectly spelt member details. We have been engaging our desktop tracing team who have presented evidence of inaccurate data received. The issue is that we are still getting some wrong information from ID numbers we got allocated which in turn delays the tracing process as we are forced to clean up the data multiple times.

Recoupment ZDM would like to seek clarity on how the fund is recouping as we invoice based on SLA agreed upon amounts. To date we have not seen any payments reflective of the recoupment amount as per the SLA. Kind regards CEO Vatiswa Ndakisa
